posts.text:
take 128 seconds and watch this - and pass it on

Video

It’s good that everyone now gets that most elections are auctions. The election conversation is now about who got outspent by whom. It’s good that nobody pretends most elections are anything other than bidding wars. Acknowledging this systemic problem is a step toward fixing it.
Bumped into my pal Colorado AG @pweiser at the Nuggets-Sixers game — love his push to expose the corruption around the Ticketmaster merger
taking my Philly dad to the Nuggets-Sixers game tonight — got here early to get the Christian Braun bobblehead giveaway

A presidency with no limits isn’t hypothetical anymore.  So what do you do about it?  

Tomorrow evening join me, @staceyabrams, Jared Jacang Maher and leaders from MoveOn, Indivisible, and Public Citizen for a virtual @LeverNews event on what comes next.


The Lever (@LeverNews)

Tomorrow we’re hosting the biggest live event in The Lever’s history with @staceyabrams @davidsirota and leaders from MoveOn, Indivisible, and Public Citizen.

Join us as we break down how we got here—and what comes ne

I like this video & find it entertaining.

I also think that if we understand attention to be a finite & very valuable political resource, it's bad that Obama mostly uses the disproportionate resources he has to turn our attention away from - rather than combat - societal crises.

Literally, he is one of the very few people who large numbers of people are willing to hear political ideas from. And yet save for the occasional campaign appearance toward the end of election cycles, he mostly spends

Gov. @JanetMillsforME is suddenly running ads trying to make the Maine Senate race about random Reddit threads rather than about her literally vetoing Democratic bills in order to help corporate fat cats crush working-class voters.

If you think the “all-powerful president” thing is new, it’s not. In 1973, Nixon was openly saying a president can’t be constrained by Congress — especially on war — because modern conflicts require speed, not debate. 

Episode 1 of Master Plan: The Kingmakers goes back to that earlier constitutional showdown, when fears of a kinglike presidency were already colliding with arguments for expanding presidential power.

Video

🚨MASTER PLAN SEASON 2 IS HERE 🚨 

Unearthing never-before-seen documents, we expose the secret plot to turn the presidency into a king.

Trump is the culmination of a 50-year plan that spanned presidencies of both parties.

Watch here & pass it on👇

Video
Listen here: levernews.com/the-kingmakers…
Join the event here: www.youtube.com/watch?v=KOUN...

A presidency with no limits isn’t hypothetical anymore.

So what do you do about it?

On March 18, join me, @staceyabrams, Jared Jacang Maher and leaders from MoveOn, Indivisible, and Public Citizen for a live virtual @LeverNews event on what comes next.
Listen here: www.levernews.com/the-kingmake...

In 1973, Nixon was openly saying a president can’t be constrained by Congress on war — because modern conflicts require speed, not debate. 

Episode 1 of Master Plan: The Kingmakers opens with that earlier constitutional clash.

🚨MASTER PLAN SEASON 2 IS HERE🚨 

Unearthing never-before-seen documents, we expose the secret plot to turn the presidency into a king.

Trump is the culmination of a 50-year plan.

You may think you know the story, but you don't. It's been secret - until now. 

Watch & PLEASE repost to pass it on👇
I suspect most Americans at this point know this truth in their gut - and that's why so many are so disillusioned and feel the entire society is rigged.

In almost every industry & endeavor, success/victory is exclusively for those who can outright buy the algorithms, the infrastructure, the platforms and the market share - regardless of smarts, hustle, creativity or any other intangible (I'm surprised the algorithm even let you read this post).
This slim possibility basically no longer exists in an America where today almost nothing can be Moneyballed at scale.

Moneyball is an appealing story is because it harkens back to a slightly more meritocratic America where something other than money - smarts, hustle, creativity, etc. - could overcome money & lead to success.

A short 🧵

Listen to THE KINGMAKERS, Ep 1: After The Fall Of The Imperial President

www.levernews.com/the-kingmake...

Before today’s fears of a kinglike president, there was Nixon.

In 1973, he vetoed the War Powers Act and argued that limits on presidential authority were dangerous. Decades later, Trump said Article II lets him do “whatever I want as president.”

That is the story of Master Plan: The Kingmakers.
Listen to THE KINGMAKERS, Ep 1: After The Fall Of The Imperial President here: www.levernews.com/the-kingmake...

“The restrictions… upon the authority of the President are both unconstitutional and dangerous to the best interests of our Nation.”
— Nixon vetoing the War Powers Act, 1973

Congress overrode his veto and tried to rein in the “imperial presidency.” But backlash helped build the current presidency.

🚨MASTER PLAN SEASON 2 IS HERE🚨 

Unearthing never-before-seen documents, we expose the secret plot to turn the presidency into a king.

Trump is the culmination of a 50-year plan.

You may think you know the story, but you don't. It's been secret - until now. 

Watch & PLEASE repost to pass it on👇
Listen now: masterplanpodcast.com

The road to a kinglike presidency starter decades before Donal Trump.

Episode 1 of Master Plan: The Kingmakers traces the roots of the modern all-powerful presidency back to Nixon, Watergate, Cambodia, and the “imperial presidency.”

Ahead of the launch of Season 2 of our audio series Master Plan, I talked to C-Span about my life in politics/journalism - from my first job on Capitol Hill with Bernie Sanders to the 2020 campaign to launching @levernews.com from scratch.

The episode is here: www.c-span.org/podcasts/sub...

There was a hugely important moment on TV last night. 

When oligarchs start warning about oligarchy, it’s a sign that all the work to shift the Overton Window hasn’t been in vain.

The big question now is whether Dems can be forced to respond to the opportunity.

sirota.substack.com/p/when-oliga...

Illinois Gov. @jbpritzker.bsky.social does an excellent job here of explaining the need to tax social media companies so that they pay for the very serious harms their extractive business model is imposing on society. Good question & great answer from Pritzker. Impressive.
THE KINGMAKERS, Ep 1: After The Fall Of The Imperial President . Season 2 of the Master Plan podcast begins fifty years before today’s fears about a kinglike president.
LEVER WEEKLY: Secret Agent, Man  (by The Lever). Foreign-influence schemes, AI seat assignments, and more from The Lever this week.
YOU LOVE TO SEE IT: Go Ahead, Just Look Up!  (by Lyna Bentahar). A spacecraft crashes for mankind, lawmakers take gunmakers to court, broadband access gets broader, and a big state buys up bad debt.
Where Is The Pentagon’s Money Going?  (by Lyna Bentahar). Test your knowledge of the Iran war, entertainment monopolies, and other issues that really matter.

Donate TEst  (by Nick Byron Campbell). Donate To The Lever

Tip Jar





Help Us Do More Stories Like This

The Epstein Prosecutor With A Portfolio Problem  (by Freddy Brewster). As Trump’s top Wall Street prosecutor, Jay Clayton is spearheading Epstein and Venezuela legal efforts — while holding investments in companies with a stake in such matters.
Utilities Cash In On America’s Energy Crisis  (by Veronica Riccobene). Profits hit $186 billion between 2021 and 2024 as electricity and gas margins outperformed other industries.
New War, Same Neocons  (by Katya Schwenk). The new Iran war follows a road map drafted by a revived neocon network of Iraq War defense hawks, Israel lobby allies, and dark money groups.
Did Iran Just Launch A Shadow War On American Soil? . Trump attacked Iran. That’s when a mysterious radio broadcast in Farsi began.
No Kings: One Year Later…Where Do We Go From Here?  (by The Lever). Together, we’ll tackle the question facing the country right now: How do you confront a presidency that operates without limits?

CVS Threatens Tennessee Over Antitrust Crackdown  (by Veronica Riccobene). The conglomerate has pledged to close all 134 of its locations in the state if lawmakers attempt to rein in its sprawling health business.
The Only Way To Stop Trump’s Mad War . Trump’s Iran war is the culmination of a decades-long master plan — and only Congress has the power to end it.
War As An Investment Strategy  (by Veronica Riccobene). Private equity is looking to get its cut of the wartime economy.

The Miami Trial Exposing Foreign Influence In Trumpland  (by Luke Goldstein). Inside a $50 million Venezuelan lobbying deal and a court battle ensnaring White House power brokers — including Secretary of State Marco Rubio and chief of staff Susie Wiles.
How To Save The World  (by Veronica Riccobene). Here’s how Congress can stop Trump from initiating nuclear armageddon.

Did Iran Just Launch A Shadow War On American Soil? (With Shane Harris). When American bombs dropped on Iran, a mysterious shortwave radio station began broadcasting a list of numbers in Farsi across the globe. Reminiscent of Cold War spy communications, the broadcast raises serious questions: Where is the broadcast coming from, and who is listening to it? Is Iran sendin

Who Sold America On The Iran War? (With Hooman Majd & Ryan Grim). On February 28, the United States and Israel began bombing Iran — with no congressional declaration, public debate, or clear endgame. For those who grew up watching the Ayatollah cast as America’s original supervillain on the nightly news, it wasn’t exactly a surprise. Donald Trump pitched the idea

The Court Disarmed Trump’s Tariff Weapon. Who Will Pay? (With Lori Wallach). On Friday, the Supreme Court ruled that President Donald Trump’s tariffs were illegal, striking a blow to his favorite geopolitical strategy. The decision could make it far harder for any president to wield tariffs as a weapon, but how will the ruling affect your wallet?

Today on Lever Time, David

He Sent One Email. Then Federal Agents Came To His Door. . In October, a Philadelphia man emailed an attorney for the Department of Homeland Security, criticizing a government policy. Hours later, federal agents and local police were banging on his door to interrogate him without a warrant. It feels like something out of a movie, but it's real.

When Disaster, Inc. Comes To Town (With Katya Schwenk). In February 2023, a Norfolk Southern freight train derailed in East Palestine, Ohio, setting off a chemical disaster. Politicians and lawyers promised justice, and the railroad company swiftly settled for a historic $600 million. 

Three years later, many victims haven’t seen a dime. Where did all t

What Tech Wants: God In The Machine. While one tech CEO warns that artificial intelligence could “destroy all life on Earth,” other industry giants have declared war on rules that could limit AI’s power. Their battlefield? The 2026 midterm elections.

In Part Three of What Tech Wants, Lever Time’s investigation into why Silicon Valley

What Tech Wants: Crypto Reign Of Terror (with Jacob Silverman and Molly White). In 2024, the cryptocurrency industry spent more than $130 million to influence elections nationwide. Their campaign donations and lobbying efforts turned the tables for their risky boom-and-bust business, which is now poised to overtake our entire financial system.

In Part Two of What Tech Wants, L

What Tech Wants: The San Francisco Experiment. In 2022, nonprofit neighborhood groups, backed by Silicon Valley billionaires, helped recall a progressive district attorney in San Francisco. What happened next would change the country. 

In Part One of What Tech Wants, Lever Time’s investigation into why Silicon Valley is buying our democracy, pr

They Banned Parents From Recording Their Kids' Games. We Changed That.. As private investment companies encroach upon youth sports, how does that hurt the players — and their families? As Lever reporter Luke Goldstein discovered, there’s more at stake than just higher prices.
